title: mapTrigger
text: mapTrigger Maps DSP triggers to events on DOM elements. mapTrigger(triggerName) Arguments triggerName The name of the trigger (string) for which a mapping object will be returned. Exceptions None Return value Returns a mapping object, an array of objects, null, or undefined. Description The framework calls the mapTrigger() function for every (web) trigger defined in the IDE for the bound field, occurrence, collection, or component. To create a mapping for the named trigger, the mapTrigger() function should return an object or an array of objects similar to the following format: Explain this code { element: <DOM element>, event_name: <name of event>, validate: false | true } OR [ { element: <DOM element1>, event_name: <name of event1>, validate: false | true }, { element: <DOM element2>, event_name: <name of event2>, validate: false | true } ] Copy Whenever the named event occurs on the specified DOM element, the framework will first get the widget value and validates it (if so indicated). On successful validation, the trigger is fired. If the mapTrigger() function does not returns a mapping object, the trigger is not fired. Note: The DOM event is not propagated when it causes the trigger execution. Note: The validation-related API is not fired for collection, occurrence, and component widgets. Therefore, the validate parameter should be false for these widget types. Note: System triggers cannot be declared at the Public Web access level and therefore cannot be invoked from a DSP client. As a result, you cannot use mapTrigger() to map a DSP system trigger. Example Explain this code mapTrigger(triggerName) { this.log("mapTrigger", triggerName); let triggerMapping; switch (triggerName) { case "onchange": triggerMapping = [ { element: this.elements.input, event_name: "change", validate: true }, { element: this.elements.button, event_name: "click", validate: true }]; break; } return triggerMapping; } Copy Validation parameters The validation parameters in the object returned by mapTrigger() has different meanings: Widget Type Validate value Behavior Field true Framework calls getValue() and validate() before trigger execution Field false Trigger executes immediately without validation Collection false validation not supported at this level Occurrence false validation not supported at this level Component false validation not supported at this level Note: Validation-related API functions ( getValue , validate , showError , hideError ) are only fired for field-level widgets. Trigger parameters Uniface UX widgets support IN parameters for triggers and webtriggers, enabling these triggers to receive input data through IN parameters. As with operations, only scalar data types are supported to ensure consistent and reliable data handling. UX widgets can pass parameters to a trigger or webtrigger via the optional parameter_callback property in the mapping object(s) returned by mapTrigger() . The parameter_callback property must be a function that returns an array of scalar values to pass as parameters to the trigger. Explain this code { element: <DOM element>, event_name: <name of event>, validate: false | true, parameter_callback: function() { return <array of argument values>; } } Copy Example Explain this code mapTrigger(triggerName) { this.log("mapTrigger", triggerName); let triggerMapping; switch (triggerName) { case "detail": triggerMapping = [ { element: this.elements.button, event_name: "click", validate: true, parameter_callback: get_params }]; break; } return triggerMapping; } function get_params() { return ["John", "Peterson"]; } Copy In the DSP button widget, define a detail trigger with two input parameters: Explain this code trigger detail public web throws params string p1: IN string p2: IN endparams webmessage "param1=%%p1%%% param2=%%p2%%%" return 0 end Copy Was this topic helpful?
